title: Same-lap pit-wave context
summary: Use current-lap `PitStop` rates among other cars to describe active pit waves.
rationale: Pit stops often cluster, and the run explored current-lap pit-wave context. Because `PitStop` is a current-row feature in test, same-lap leave-one-out peer counts are target-free for next-lap prediction.
implementation_hint: Group by race-year/race and `LapNumber`; compute leave-one-out counts and rates of other cars with `PitStop` active. Interact these with current tyre pressure, late-race flags, and different-fresh-slick feasibility.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Contemporaneous field features assume the full lap snapshot is available at inference. If deployed online before all cars complete the lap, this would be invalid.
